reasoning: Sodium 2-mercaptoethanolate is the sodium salt of 2-mercaptoethanol (HOCH2CH2SH), where the thiol proton is replaced by sodium, making it an organosulfur compound with an alkylthiolate character. It fits under thiols/alkylthiols as the core functional group is an aliphatic thiol (deprotonated form as sodium salt).
